metrics.reason: Shows the current active state (running, exited, failed, etc.) of the service that triggered the alert.
Indicates whether the unit file was successfully loaded; a bad load state can prevent the service from starting.
Shows the enable/disable state of the unit file; a disabled or masked unit cannot be started automatically.
Provides the time the service has been up; a very short uptime before failure can hint at rapid crashes.
Returns detailed unit properties (ExecStart, Restart settings, etc.) useful for diagnosing misconfiguration.
Overall CPU utilization; high CPU pressure can cause services to fail to start or be killed.
1‑minute load average; spikes may indicate resource contention affecting the service.
5‑minute load average for trend analysis of sustained load.
15‑minute load average to see longer‑term load patterns.
Percentage of used RAM; low memory can cause the service to be OOM‑killed.
Free swap space; insufficient swap can exacerbate memory pressure.
Host uptime; helps correlate service failure with recent reboots or kernel updates.
Incoming interface errors; network issues may prevent the service from binding to ports.
Outgoing interface errors; useful if the service communicates externally.
I/O utilization of the primary disk; high I/O can delay service start‑up or cause timeouts.
I/O utilization of the secondary disk; same rationale as above.
